property.name: log.dir
property.description: Log directory for Ranger Audit Server service

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.webapp.dir
property.description: Path to the extracted webapp directory (relative to AUDIT_SERVER_HOME_DIR). Must be extracted directory, not WAR file, because Jersey cannot scan packages in unexpanded WAR files.

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.host
property.description: - In Docker: Use full service name with domain (e.g., ranger-audit-ingestor.rangernw) - In VM: Use the actual FQDN (e.g., ranger.example.com)

property.name: hadoop.security.authentication
property.description: Hadoop authentication type. Must be set to "kerberos" for EmbeddedServer to call UGI.loginUserFromKeytab() and load principal credentials for SPNEGO authentication.

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.kerberos.enabled
property.description: Enable Kerberos authentication for incoming REST API requests

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.kerberos.type
property.description: Authentication type: kerberos or simple

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.kerberos.principal
property.description: INCOMING: SPNEGO HTTP principal for authenticating REST API requests FROM plugins. This principal must be present in the keytab file.

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.kerberos.keytab
property.description: Keytab file containing HTTP principal for SPNEGO authentication

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.kerberos.name.rules
property.description: Kerberos name rules for principal mapping

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.bind.address
property.description: Hostname for Kerberos principal _HOST resolution in HTTP principal - Example: If set to "myhost.example.com" and principal is "HTTP/_HOST@REALM", it becomes "HTTP/myhost.example.com@REALM" - In Docker: Use full service name with domain (e.g., ranger-audit-ingestor.rangernw) - In VM: Use the actual FQDN (e.g., ranger.example.com) - MUST match the hostname in the keytab principal

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.cookie.path
property.description: Cookie path for delegation token

property.name: config.prefix
property.description: Configuration prefix for JWT authentication

property.name: ranger.audit.jwt.auth.enabled
property.description: Enable JWT authentication for audit REST API

property.name: ranger.audit.jwt.auth.provider-url
property.description: JWKS provider URL for JWT token validation

property.name: ranger.audit.jwt.auth.public-key
property.description: JWT public key in PEM format (alternative to provider-url)

property.name: ranger.audit.jwt.auth.cookie.name
property.description: Name of the cookie containing JWT token

property.name: ranger.audit.jwt.auth.audiences
property.description: Expected audiences for JWT validation (comma-separated)

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.service.dev_hdfs.allowed.users
property.description: Allowed users for dev_hdfs (Policy Manager service name; from policy.download.auth.users)

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.service.dev_yarn.allowed.users
property.description: Allowed users for dev_yarn (YARN RM/NM)

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.service.dev_hive.allowed.users
property.description: Allowed users for dev_hive (HiveServer2 + Metastore)

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.service.dev_hbase.allowed.users
property.description: Allowed users for dev_hbase (HBase Master + RegionServer)

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.service.dev_kafka.allowed.users
property.description: Allowed users for dev_kafka

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.service.dev_knox.allowed.users
property.description: Allowed users for dev_knox

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.service.dev_kms.allowed.users
property.description: Allowed users for dev_kms

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.service.dev_trino.allowed.users
property.description: Allowed users for dev_trino

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.service.dev_ozone.allowed.users
property.description: Allowed users for dev_ozone (OM, SCM, DataNode)

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.service.dev_solr.allowed.users
property.description: Allowed users for dev_solr (Solr plugin)

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.service.dev_tag.allowed.users
property.description: Allowed users for dev_tag (TagSync service)

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.service.dev_atlas.allowed.users
property.description: Allowed users for dev_atlas (Atlas)

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.service.dev_kudu.allowed.users
property.description: Allowed users for dev_kudu (Kudu master/tserver)

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.service.dev_nifi.allowed.users
property.description: Allowed users for dev_nifi (NiFi)

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.auth.to.local
property.description:
Kerberos auth_to_local rules for mapping authenticated principals to service names. Uses Hadoop KerberosName syntax to convert full Kerberos principals to short usernames checked against ranger.audit.ingestor.service.<repo>.allowed.users (Policy Manager policy.download.auth.users).
IMPORTANT: These rules only apply to Kerberos/SPNEGO authentication. JWT and basic auth usernames are already in short form and pass through unchanged.
Default rules provided: - RULE:[2:$1/$2@$0]([ndj]n/.*@.*|hdfs/.*@.*)s/.*/hdfs/ # nn,dn,jn,hdfs/* -> hdfs (dev_hdfs) - RULE:[2:$1/$2@$0]([rn]m/.*@.*|yarn/.*@.*)s/.*/yarn/ # rm,nm,yarn/* -> yarn (dev_yarn) - RULE:[2:$1/$2@$0](jhs/.*@.*)s/.*/mapred/ # jhs/* -> mapred - RULE:[2:$1/$2@$0](hive/.*@.*)s/.*/hive/ # hive/* -> hive (dev_hive) - RULE:[2:$1/$2@$0](hbase/.*@.*)s/.*/hbase/ # hbase/* -> hbase (dev_hbase) - RULE:[2:$1/$2@$0](kafka/.*@.*)s/.*/kafka/ # kafka/* -> kafka (dev_kafka) - RULE:[2:$1/$2@$0](knox/.*@.*)s/.*/knox/ # knox/* -> knox (dev_knox) - RULE:[2:$1/$2@$0](rangerkms/.*@.*)s/.*/rangerkms/ # rangerkms/* -> rangerkms (dev_kms) - RULE:[2:$1/$2@$0](trino/.*@.*)s/.*/trino/ # trino/* -> trino (dev_trino) - RULE:[2:$1/$2@$0](ozone/.*@.*)s/.*/ozone/ # ozone/* -> ozone (dev_ozone) - RULE:[2:$1/$2@$0](om/.*@.*)s/.*/om/ # om/* -> om (dev_ozone) - RULE:[2:$1/$2@$0](scm/.*@.*)s/.*/scm/ # scm/* -> scm (dev_ozone) - RULE:[2:$1/$2@$0](dn/.*@.*)s/.*/dn/ # dn/* -> dn (dev_ozone) - RULE:[2:$1/$2@$0](solr/.*@.*)s/.*/solr/ # solr/* -> solr (dev_solr) - RULE:[2:$1/$2@$0](rangertagsync/.*@.*)s/.*/rangertagsync/ # rangertagsync/* -> rangertagsync (dev_tag) - RULE:[2:$1/$2@$0](atlas/.*@.*)s/.*/atlas/ # atlas/* -> atlas (dev_atlas) - RULE:[2:$1/$2@$0](kudu/.*@.*)s/.*/kudu/ # kudu/* -> kudu (dev_kudu) - RULE:[2:$1/$2@$0](nifi/.*@.*)s/.*/nifi/ # nifi/* -> nifi (dev_nifi) - RULE:[1:$1@$0](.*@.*)s/@.*// # user@REALM -> user - DEFAULT

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.service.kerberos.principal
property.description: rangerauditserver user kerberos principal for authentication into kafka

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.service.kerberos.keytab
property.description: keytab of the rangerauditserver principal

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.kafka.bootstrap.servers
property.description: Kafka broker hosts for Kafka audit destination. example: kafka-broker1:9092,kafka-broker2:9092,kafka-broker3:9092 In Docker, use the service name of the kafka brokers: ranger-kafka.rangernw:9092

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.kafka.partitioner.class
property.description: Kafka partitioner class for distributing audit messages across partitions. Default: org.apache.ranger.audit.producer.kafka.AuditPartitioner (plugin-based partitioning) Note: If configured.plugins is not set, uses Kafka's default hash-based partitioner instead. Can be overridden with custom implementation of org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.Partitioner

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.kafka.configured.plugins
property.description: Comma-separated list of configured plugin IDs. If set: Uses AuditPartitioner with auto-calculated partitions (sum of plugin allocations + buffer). If empty/not set: Uses Kafka default hash-based partitioner with topic.partitions value. Each plugin receives dedicated partitions based on topic.partitions.per.configured.plugin (default: 3).

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.kafka.topic.partitions.per.configured.plugin
property.description: Default number of partitions per configured plugin. Can be overridden per plugin using plugin.partition.overrides.{pluginName} properties. Used ONLY when configured.plugins is set (for plugin-based partitioning). Ignored when configured.plugins is empty.

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.kafka.topic.partitions.buffer
property.description: Number of buffer partitions reserved for unconfigured plugins. Used ONLY when configured.plugins is set (for plugin-based partitioning). Total = (sum of plugin partitions) + (buffer partitions). Example: 7 plugins × 3 + 9 buffer = 30 total. Ignored when configured.plugins is empty.

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.kafka.topic.partitions
property.description: Number of partitions for the Kafka audit topic. Used ONLY when configured.plugins is NOT set (empty) for hash-based partitioning. When configured.plugins is set, partitions are auto-calculated from plugin configuration. Default: 10 partitions (sufficient for small-medium deployments) Recommendations for hash-based mode: - Small deployments (1-3 consumers): 10 partitions - Medium deployments (4-10 consumers): 20-30 partitions - Large deployments (10+ consumers): 50+ partitions

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.kafka.replication.factor
property.description: Replication factor for the Kafka audit topic. Default: 3 (production with multiple brokers) Development/Single broker: 1 (current setting for Docker environment) Must not exceed the number of available Kafka brokers.

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.kafka.security.protocol
property.description: Security protocol for Kafka audit destination. example: PLAINTEXT for non-Kerberos mode, SASL_PLAINTEXT for Kerberos mode, SASL_SSL for SSL mode

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.kafka.request.timeout.ms
property.description: Kafka producer and AdminClient request timeout (ms)

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.kafka.producer.batch.size
property.description: Kafka batch.size in bytes. Default 128 KiB (65536–262144 for high volume).

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.kafka.producer.linger.ms
property.description: Wait up to this many ms to coalesce records into larger batches (10–50 typical).

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.kafka.producer.buffer.memory
property.description: Producer buffer memory in bytes. Default 128 MiB.

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.kafka.producer.compression.type
property.description: Compression for audit JSON (lz4 recommended: low CPU, good ratio).

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.kafka.producer.delivery.timeout.ms
property.description: Upper bound on producer delivery attempts including retries (ms).

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.kafka.producer.max.request.size
property.description: Max size of a produce request; must be less than broker message.max.bytes.

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.kafka.producer.max.block.ms
property.description: Max time producer blocks when buffer is full (backpressure).

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.kafka.producer.batch.send.timeout.ms
property.description: Application timeout waiting for REST batch send callbacks (ms).

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.recovery.enabled
property.description: Enable audit message recovery system. When enabled, failed audit messages are written to local spool files and retried to Kafka automatically.

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.recovery.spool.dir
property.description: Directory for storing .failed files with audit messages that failed to send to Kafka. Files remain here until successfully processed, then moved to archive as .processed files.

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.recovery.archive.dir
property.description: Directory for successfully processed files. Files are moved here with .processed extension after all messages are successfully retried to Kafka. Old .processed files are automatically deleted based on retention policy (max.processed.files).

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.recovery.file.rotation.interval.sec
property.description: Time interval in seconds for rotating spool files. Default is 300 seconds (5 minutes). A new spool file is created after this interval.

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.recovery.max.messages.per.file
property.description: Maximum number of messages to write per spool file before rotation. File is closed and moved to archive after reaching this limit.

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.recovery.retry.interval.sec
property.description: Time interval in seconds between retry attempts for archived files. Default is 60 seconds (1 minute).

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.recovery.archive.max.processed.files
property.description: Maximum number of .processed files to retain in archive directory. Oldest .processed files are deleted when this limit is exceeded. Failed messages remain in spool directory as .failed files for retry.

property.name: ranger.audit.ingestor.recovery.retry.max.attempts
property.description: Maximum number of retry attempts per message before keeping file as .failed in spool. Default is 3 attempts with exponential backoff.
